title: First Unique Character in a String
description: Given a string s, find the first non-repeating character in it and return its index. If it does not exist, return -1.

examples.explanation: The first non-repeating character is 'l', which is at index 0.
The first non-repeating character is 'v', which is at index 2.
There are no non-repeating characters in the string, so the output is -1.

constraints: You must implement an algorithm with O(n) time complexity.
The input string will have a length of at most 10^5.
hints: Consider using a hash table to store character counts.
Iterate through the string twice; first for counting, second for finding the index.
Pay attention to edge cases where all characters repeat.
Think about how to handle strings with varying character sets.
